behaviors.description: Conversation history passed to LLM is trimmed to most recent 10 messages when full history exceeds context window
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic list-slicing logic independent of LLM behavior. A unit test can mock the LLM, create a history with 15+ messages, and verify only the last 10 are passed to the model. Mocking does not hide failures in the trimming logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a conversation history with 15 messages. Mock the LLM call. Assert that the trimming function passes exactly the last 10 messages to the mocked LLM. Verify the trimmed slice is correct (messages 6-15, not 1-10 or other ranges).

behaviors.description: Generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing generic or off-topic reply
behaviors.justification: Whether a response is relevant depends entirely on real LLM behavior, which is non-deterministic. A mock LLM can always be configured to return on-topic responses, hiding real failures where the actual model produces irrelevant outputs. Catching this failure requires the real model to generate actual responses and evaluation logic to measure relevance.
behaviors.test_description: Call the system with a real LLM using 5-10 distinct customer queries across different domains (billing, technical, account). Verify each response addresses the specific question asked (not generic boilerplate). Measure relevance with a classifier or manual review. Run multiple times to account for LLM variance.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration
behaviors.justification: The tone check likely depends on an LLM or ML classifier, which is non-deterministic and failure-prone. A mocked tone checker would always return predictable results, hiding real failures where the actual classifier fails to detect formality. The regeneration also depends on the real LLM producing a tone-adjusted response. Both failures (misclassification and poor regeneration) require real agentic infrastructure to catch.
behaviors.test_description: Call the system with a real LLM using 10+ test cases: formal responses for casual-tier customers, casual responses for formal-tier customers, and correct-tone responses for each tier. Verify the tone checker correctly flags mismatches. Verify regeneration is triggered only when appropriate. Verify the regenerated response actually has the correct tone for the customer tier.

behaviors.description: Full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to database with correct timestamps
behaviors.justification: Mocking the database hides real failures: schema mismatches, insert failures, timestamp timezone bugs, or missing fields. The persistence logic requires integration with real (or test) database infrastructure. A mock would pass while a real DB insert fails. LLM and tone checker can remain mocked since the behavior is about persistence logic, not LLM quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local test database. Mock the LLM to return a known response and the tone checker to either pass or trigger regeneration. Execute the full chat flow. Query the database directly and verify: (1) all fields are present and correctly populated, (2) user message, response, and any regeneration are all recorded, (3) timestamps are correct and in the expected timezone, (4) the exchange record ID links all parts correctly.

behaviors.description: When tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft
behaviors.justification: This behavior involves multiple agentic steps in sequence: (1) real LLM generates response, (2) real tone classifier evaluates it, (3) conditional real LLM regeneration if needed, (4) application logic returns the correct response. Each step is non-deterministic; mocking any of them hides real failures. Catching failures requires the full agentic pipeline to execute: tone check must correctly identify the problem, regeneration must produce a better response, and both must flow through application logic correctly. This is a multi-step workflow where failures cascade.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the chatbot with a real LLM and tone classifier using 5+ test cases where: (1) initial response is flagged as incorrect tone by the real classifier, (2) regeneration is triggered, (3) the regenerated response is captured. Verify the customer-facing API returns only the regenerated response (not the initial one). Verify the database stores both draft and final response but the API exposes only the final. Verify tone of regenerated response matches customer tier using real evaluation.
